coredata.dc:title: Formation of hard carbon derived from sulfonated pitch as advanced electrodes for lithium-ion batteries
coredata.prism:copyright: Copyright © 2020 The Authors. Published by ESG. Published by Elsevier B.V.
coredata.prism:publisher: The Authors. Published by ESG. Published by Elsevier B.V.
coredata.dc:creator: Zuo, Jialu
Guo, Yan
coredata.dc:description: The exploration for low-cost and sustainable anode materials plays a key role in the future of lithium-ion batteries (LIBs). Sulfonated pitch-based hard carbon obtained by using high-temperature calcination of industrial sulfonated pitch as a raw material is evaluated as an anode material for LIBs. Experimental results show that sulfonated pitch-based hard carbon exhibits excellent electrochemical performance as an anode for LIBs. After 50 cycles, the discharge capacity is still at 310 mAh g-1 with a capacity retention rate of 83.8% under a current density of 0.1C. The low-cost and facile synthesis process of sulfonated pitch-based hard carbon makes it a promising anode material for LIBs in the future.
coredata.dcterms:subject: lithium ion batteries
hard carbon
sulfonated pitch

xocs:meta.xocs:copyright-line: Copyright © 2020 The Authors. Published by ESG. Published by Elsevier B.V.

xocs:doc.xocs:rawtext: Short Communication Formation of hard carbon derived from sulfonated pitch as advanced electrodes for lithium-ion batteries Jialu Zuo, Yan Guo, guoyan@imu.edu.cn College of Chemistry and Chemical Engineering, Inner Mongolia University, Hohhot 010021, P. R. China College of Chemistry and Chemical Engineering Inner Mongolia University Hohhot 010021 P. R. China College of Chemistry and Chemical Engineering, Inner Mongolia University, Hohhot 010021, P. R. China The exploration for low-cost and sustainable anode materials plays a key role in the future of lithium-ion batteries (LIBs). Sulfonated pitch-based hard carbon obtained by using high-temperature calcination of industrial sulfonated pitch as a raw material is evaluated as an anode material for LIBs. Experimental results show that sulfonated pitch-based hard carbon exhibits excellent electrochemical performance as an anode for LIBs. After 50 cycles, the discharge capacity is still at 310 mAh g-1 with a capacity retention rate of 83.8% under a current density of 0.1C. The low-cost and facile synthesis process of sulfonated pitch-based hard carbon makes it a promising anode material for LIBs in the future.Keywords lithium ion batteries hard carbon sulfonated pitch References 1. X. Lin, X. Du, P.S. Tsui, J.-Q. Huang, H. Tan, B. Zhang, Electrochim. Acta 316: (2019) 60-X. Lin, X. Du, P.S. Tsui, J.-Q. Huang, H. Tan, B. Zhang, Electrochim. Acta, 316 (2019) 60. 2. H.B. Wu, J.S. Chen, X.W. Lou, H.H. Hng, Nanoscale 3: (2011) 4082-H.B. Wu, J.S. Chen, X.W. Lou, H.H. Hng, Nanoscale, 3 (2011) 4082. 3. S.L. Candelaria, Y. Shao, W. Zhou, X. Li, J. Xiao, J.-G. Zhang, Y. Wang, J. simple-head.ce:title: Formation of hard carbon derived from sulfonated pitch as advanced electrodes for lithium-ion batteries

ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: College of Chemistry and Chemical Engineering, Inner Mongolia University, Hohhot 010021, P. R. China

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: College of Chemistry and Chemical Engineering

ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: College of Chemistry and Chemical Engineering, Inner Mongolia University, Hohhot 010021, P. R. China

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: The exploration for low-cost and sustainable anode materials plays a key role in the future of lithium-ion batteries (LIBs). Sulfonated pitch-based hard carbon obtained by using high-temperature calcination of industrial sulfonated pitch as a raw material is evaluated as an anode material for LIBs. Experimental results show that sulfonated pitch-based hard carbon exhibits excellent electrochemical performance as an anode for LIBs. After 50 cycles, the discharge capacity is still at 310 mAh g-1 with a capacity retention rate of 83.8% under a current density of 0.1C. The low-cost and facile synthesis process of sulfonated pitch-based hard carbon makes it a promising anode material for LIBs in the future.
